title: Don't create directory on container
body:
In a prior PR, we were creating the directory from the
translated symlink onto the host container.  Instead
try to create that in the container and if it exists
already, just continue.

Fixes: #1562

Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

comments.body: FYI: @karelyatin , thanks again for the eagle eye and heads up.

@nalind & @rhatdan I've pushed a new commit and the tests don't seem to be running.  I think this is what you were referring to during standup today?  I'm running the bud.bats on my vm, holler if you know of a way to retest this.

@TomSweeneyRedHat the tests are firing (the latest, which seems to match the current tip, is at https://travis-ci.org/containers/buildah/jobs/526947455), but you're hitting the problem that #1545 also hit, which is our frequent attempts to pull `docker.io/kubernetes/pause` images.  The patch on the tip of that PR should help.
Maybe I'm being oblivious, but is #1565 the right PR?  I'm only seeing a small doc change on that one and only one commit.
OK, that one makes more sense, thanks!  I've done all the kubernetes -> ki8s.gcr changes in this PR on my sandbox, should I push it over or wait for yours to merge and rebase?
Alright, the test failed again, I've pulled the test changes over from #1564 and pushed a new commit over.  I've gotta head out, but will be back on-line in a bit.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 4bb6252) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
rebased and repushed, fingers tightly crossed for Travis/homu....

Testing: 

```

Getting image source signatures

STEP 2: RUN mkdir /var/lib/tempest

STEP 4: WORKDIR /tempest
STEP 5: RUN touch /etc/notareal.conf
STEP 6: RUN chmod 664 /etc/notareal.conf
STEP 7: COPY Dockerfile.baddir /tempest

Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> 36a1873f1ebf610a5a021855cfda0572c9cf95bea1c6ca21a68c8b65bf6b81bd

[root@localhost buildah]# buildah from --name myctr tom
myctr

[root@localhost buildah]# buildah run myctr /bin/sh
/var/lib/tempest # ls
Dockerfile.baddir

/var/lib/tempest # cat Dockerfile.baddir
FROM alpine
RUN mkdir /var/lib/tempest
RUN ln -sf /var/lib/tempest /tempest
WORKDIR /tempest
RUN touch /etc/notareal.conf
RUN chmod 664 /etc/notareal.conf
COPY /root/Dockerfile.baddir /tempest

/var/lib/tempest # ls -alF /tempest
lrwxrwxrwx    1 root     root            16 May  1 21:01 /tempest -> /var/lib/tempest/

# ls -alF /etc/notareal.conf
-rw-rw-r--    1 root     root             0 May  1 21:01 /etc/notareal.conf

```

:pushpin: Commit 7b6fd7f has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan 7b6fd7fe419893be6901d73755c5f004d8f3e115 -->

:sunny: Test successful - [status-papr](https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/buildah/78fb869817d04675930818a2263ead54fa39add1.1556747224700041728/index.html), [status-travis](https://travis-ci.org/containers/buildah/builds/527054737?utm_source=github_status&utm_medium=notification)
Approved by: rhatdan
Pushing 78fb869817d04675930818a2263ead54fa39add1 to master...

@TomSweeneyRedHat Thanks this patch is working and properly handling WORKDIR as symlink and not creating any directory on host path.

So noticed one more issue not sure it's likely to be supported(dangling symlink, symlink pointing to non existing directory), NOTE: it works with docker build.
It no longer exist for our use case as we no longer using dangling symlink post:- https://review.opendev.org/#/c/652937/:-
Just in case looking to fix: your last patch with Resolvesymlink + my comments was handling this Use case.
[zuul@standalone ~]$ cat alpine/Dockerfile 
FROM alpine
#RUN mkdir /var/lib/tempest
RUN ln -sf /var/lib/tempest /tempest
WORKDIR /tempest
RUN touch /etc/notareal.conf
RUN chmod 664 /etc/notareal.conf
[zuul@standalone ~]$ sudo /usr/local/bin/buildah bud --tls-verify=False  -t alpine:be90d93c3c5f77f428d12a9a8a2ef97b9dada8f3_5b53d5ba alpine
STEP 1: FROM alpine
STEP 2: RUN ln -sf /var/lib/tempest /tempest
STEP 3: WORKDIR /tempest
STEP 4: Target Path: /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/6b4bcaf5f33d4a85cd92d2a19ae9db4a4b655e5fcd503e87a76ff12da621a89c/merged/tempest
STEP 5: RUN touch /etc/notareal.conf
STEP 6: Target Path: /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/6b4bcaf5f33d4a85cd92d2a19ae9db4a4b655e5fcd503e87a76ff12da621a89c/merged/tempest
container_linux.go:336: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:399: container init caused \"mkdir /tempest: file exists\""
error running container: error creating container for [/bin/sh -c touch /etc/notareal.conf]: : exit status 1
error building at STEP "RUN touch /etc/notareal.conf": error while running runtime: exit status 1
[zuul@standalone ~]$ /usr/local/bin/buildah --version
buildah version 1.9.0-dev (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)
Thanks @karelyatin , will look into it.  So I don't lose the issue, I've created #1569
